description: A regional body of salt water, or the ocean water beside a coast, takes the name 'moriwhe'. Seas are often partly bounded by land, but their names and limits follow geography and convention. 'thaluro' (ocean) names the connected global body or one of its major divisions.
articulatory_notes: With the lips closed, send voiced 'm' through the nose into middle-height rounded 'o'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed high-front 'i'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for unvoiced 'wh', then let breath continue into middle-height front 'e'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'mo.ri' is shared with 'morilu' (wave) and 'morisa' (salt). The final unvoiced rounded breath distinguishes the sea while keeping its water and salt family audible.

semantic_domains.nature: a regional saltwater body or coastal part of the ocean
semantic_domains.spatial: a named or locally bounded expanse of ocean water
